to happen, you have to first understand the fundamental
differences between traffic types and how Paid Traffic,
especially, can be a powerful driving force in growing
your business.

5
VISITOR SCENARIO EXAMPLES
Walking down the street and thought your store looked interesting enough to browse (they’re
not ready to buy, there wasn’t a product/market timing match, maybe they’ll come back later).
Walking down the street and thought your store looked interesting enough to browse
(they DO buy, there was product/market timing match).
They wanted boots, started researching (google, yellow pages) found your store and came in
and purchased.
They wanted boots, remembered your store from a previous visit, came in and purchased.
They have purchased from you before and came back for another pair (or another item).
They have purchased from you before and brought a friend in to buy a pair.

YOUR TRAFFIC SYSTEM TIP
Traffic is the ROAD
that moves people
step by step through
your customer’s
journey… no matter
where they “enter”.
Think about these six visitor scenarios and how they can be
represented within your business. If you understand why they’re
visiting, and where they’re coming from… you can automate the
sales process with multiple traffic campaigns!
What are some of the visitor scenarios for your business?

The role of 	is to move prospects

.
When applied to traffic, the Value Journey consists
of multiple marketing campaigns that work together
to move someone from “Aware” to “Promoter.”

The goal of the
Value Journey is to
ensure that we are
transforming lives.
If your product or
service doesn’t
transform your
customer for the better,
then it might not be a
product or service you
should be selling.

17
TRAFFIC TEMPERATURE
Throughout this workshop, we will categorize traffic
into one of these three temperatures: Cold, Warm,
and Hot. As the relationship builds between a
particular audience and your business, the Traffic
Temperature of that audience will also increase.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 30 --

18
CAMPAIGN OBJECTIVES
In order to run a successful paid traffic campaign,
you first need to decide what your objective is. This
will allow you to speak to a specific audience to
meet your traffic goals.
There are three specific campagin objectives:
(i.e. Introduce yourself to people who have never
heard of you before)
(i.e. Convert via entry-point offer)
(i.e. Sell a higher dollar product or repeat purchase)

20
COLD TRAFFIC
This is the NEW blood of your
business.
Introduction/Indoctrination (introduce
your brand and establish credibility).
If you have a brand new business,
you must start with cold traffic.
Pixelling (ability to follow up).
It is essential to target cold traffic,
or you’ll continue to swim in the
same pond.
Segmentation (making your follow-
up more relevant).
Cold Traffic is... 	Goals of Cold Traffic

THE BIG THREE
Google =
YouTube =
Facebook =
As you start new paid traffic campaigns and scale
existing ones, it’s important to think about how your
prospects and customers are finding you and how
you can best position yourself in front of them.
TIP
Nearly 2 billion users
are active monthly on
Facebook.
This, combined with
its easy user interface,
makes Facebook an
obvious choice when
choosing a traffic
channel, regardless of
your target market.
